=== Quick Fix Inventory ===

[D1] (11:30-11:57) TS377050: This nested call structure has a pipeable form. `addOne(5).pipe(...)` represents the same call sequence in pipe style and may be easier to read. effect(missedPipeableOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to pipe style"

[D2] (15:30-15:66) TS377050: This nested call structure has a pipeable form. `addOne(10).pipe(...)` represents the same call sequence in pipe style and may be easier to read. effect(missedPipeableOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to pipe style"

  Fix 0: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to pipe style"

[D4] (23:33-23:73) TS377050: This nested call structure has a pipeable form. `wrapInEffect(regularFunction(5)).pipe(...)` represents the same call sequence in pipe style and may be easier to read. effect(missedPipeableOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to pipe style"

[D5] (27:30-27:67) TS377050: This nested call structure has a pipeable form. `addOne(7).pipe(...)` represents the same call sequence in pipe style and may be easier to read. effect(missedPipeableOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to pipe style"

=== Quick Fix Application Results ===

=== [D1] Fix 0: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 1: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 2: "Convert to pipe style" ===

--- file:///.src/missedPipeableOpportunity.ts ---
// @effect-diagnostics missedPipeableOpportunity:warning
import { Effect } from "effect"

// Helper functions that work with Effect
const addOne = (n: number) => Effect.succeed(n + 1)
const double = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n * 2)
const multiplyBy = (n: number) => (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (m) => m * n)
const toString = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => String(n))

// Should trigger diagnostic: nested calls with innermost returning pipeable
export const shouldReport1 = addOne(5).pipe(double, toString)

// Should trigger diagnostic: 3-level nesting
const triple = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n * 3)
export const shouldReport2 = toString(triple(double(addOne(10))))

// Should report: two level nesting can benefit from pipe style
export const shouldReport4 = double(addOne(5))

// Should report, but refactor to be wrapInEffect(regularFunction(5)).pipe(double)
const regularFunction = (n: number) => n + 1
const wrapInEffect = (n: number) => Effect.succeed(n)
export const shouldNotReport2 = double(wrapInEffect(regularFunction(5)))

// Should trigger diagnostic: proper pipeable chain with correct types
const addHundred = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n + 100)
export const shouldReport3 = triple(addHundred(double(addOne(7))))

// Should NOT report: already using pipe
export const shouldNotReport3 = addOne(5).pipe(double, toString)
export const shouldNotReport4 = addOne(5).pipe(double)
export const shouldNotReport5 = addOne(5).pipe(multiplyBy(2))

// Should trigger
export const shouldReport = Effect.runPromise(Effect.log("Hello"))

=== [D2] Fix 0: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 1: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 2: "Convert to pipe style" ===

--- file:///.src/missedPipeableOpportunity.ts ---
// @effect-diagnostics missedPipeableOpportunity:warning
import { Effect } from "effect"

// Helper functions that work with Effect
const addOne = (n: number) => Effect.succeed(n + 1)
const double = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n * 2)
const multiplyBy = (n: number) => (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (m) => m * n)
const toString = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => String(n))

// Should trigger diagnostic: nested calls with innermost returning pipeable
export const shouldReport1 = toString(double(addOne(5)))

// Should trigger diagnostic: 3-level nesting
const triple = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n * 3)
export const shouldReport2 = addOne(10).pipe(double, triple, toString)

// Should report: two level nesting can benefit from pipe style
export const shouldReport4 = double(addOne(5))

// Should report, but refactor to be wrapInEffect(regularFunction(5)).pipe(double)
const regularFunction = (n: number) => n + 1
const wrapInEffect = (n: number) => Effect.succeed(n)
export const shouldNotReport2 = double(wrapInEffect(regularFunction(5)))

// Should trigger diagnostic: proper pipeable chain with correct types
const addHundred = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n + 100)
export const shouldReport3 = triple(addHundred(double(addOne(7))))

// Should NOT report: already using pipe
export const shouldNotReport3 = addOne(5).pipe(double, toString)
export const shouldNotReport4 = addOne(5).pipe(double)
export const shouldNotReport5 = addOne(5).pipe(multiplyBy(2))

// Should trigger
export const shouldReport = Effect.runPromise(Effect.log("Hello"))

=== [D3] Fix 0: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D3] Fix 1: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D3] Fix 2: "Convert to pipe style" ===

--- file:///.src/missedPipeableOpportunity.ts ---
// @effect-diagnostics missedPipeableOpportunity:warning
import { Effect } from "effect"

// Helper functions that work with Effect
const addOne = (n: number) => Effect.succeed(n + 1)
const double = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n * 2)
const multiplyBy = (n: number) => (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (m) => m * n)
const toString = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => String(n))

// Should trigger diagnostic: nested calls with innermost returning pipeable
export const shouldReport1 = toString(double(addOne(5)))

// Should trigger diagnostic: 3-level nesting
const triple = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n * 3)
export const shouldReport2 = toString(triple(double(addOne(10))))

// Should report: two level nesting can benefit from pipe style
export const shouldReport4 = addOne(5).pipe(double)

// Should report, but refactor to be wrapInEffect(regularFunction(5)).pipe(double)
const regularFunction = (n: number) => n + 1
const wrapInEffect = (n: number) => Effect.succeed(n)
export const shouldNotReport2 = double(wrapInEffect(regularFunction(5)))

// Should trigger diagnostic: proper pipeable chain with correct types
const addHundred = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n + 100)
export const shouldReport3 = triple(addHundred(double(addOne(7))))

// Should NOT report: already using pipe
export const shouldNotReport3 = addOne(5).pipe(double, toString)
export const shouldNotReport4 = addOne(5).pipe(double)
export const shouldNotReport5 = addOne(5).pipe(multiplyBy(2))

// Should trigger
export const shouldReport = Effect.runPromise(Effect.log("Hello"))

=== [D4] Fix 0: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 1: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 2: "Convert to pipe style" ===

--- file:///.src/missedPipeableOpportunity.ts ---
// @effect-diagnostics missedPipeableOpportunity:warning
import { Effect } from "effect"

// Helper functions that work with Effect
const addOne = (n: number) => Effect.succeed(n + 1)
const double = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n * 2)
const multiplyBy = (n: number) => (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (m) => m * n)
const toString = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => String(n))

// Should trigger diagnostic: nested calls with innermost returning pipeable
export const shouldReport1 = toString(double(addOne(5)))

// Should trigger diagnostic: 3-level nesting
const triple = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n * 3)
export const shouldReport2 = toString(triple(double(addOne(10))))

// Should report: two level nesting can benefit from pipe style
export const shouldReport4 = double(addOne(5))

// Should report, but refactor to be wrapInEffect(regularFunction(5)).pipe(double)
const regularFunction = (n: number) => n + 1
const wrapInEffect = (n: number) => Effect.succeed(n)
export const shouldNotReport2 = wrapInEffect(regularFunction(5)).pipe(double)

// Should trigger diagnostic: proper pipeable chain with correct types
const addHundred = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n + 100)
export const shouldReport3 = triple(addHundred(double(addOne(7))))

// Should NOT report: already using pipe
export const shouldNotReport3 = addOne(5).pipe(double, toString)
export const shouldNotReport4 = addOne(5).pipe(double)
export const shouldNotReport5 = addOne(5).pipe(multiplyBy(2))

// Should trigger
export const shouldReport = Effect.runPromise(Effect.log("Hello"))

=== [D5] Fix 0: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D5] Fix 1: "Disable missedPipeableOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D5] Fix 2: "Convert to pipe style" ===

--- file:///.src/missedPipeableOpportunity.ts ---
// @effect-diagnostics missedPipeableOpportunity:warning
import { Effect } from "effect"

// Helper functions that work with Effect
const addOne = (n: number) => Effect.succeed(n + 1)
const double = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n * 2)
const multiplyBy = (n: number) => (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (m) => m * n)
const toString = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => String(n))

// Should trigger diagnostic: nested calls with innermost returning pipeable
export const shouldReport1 = toString(double(addOne(5)))

// Should trigger diagnostic: 3-level nesting
const triple = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n * 3)
export const shouldReport2 = toString(triple(double(addOne(10))))

// Should report: two level nesting can benefit from pipe style
export const shouldReport4 = double(addOne(5))

// Should report, but refactor to be wrapInEffect(regularFunction(5)).pipe(double)
const regularFunction = (n: number) => n + 1
const wrapInEffect = (n: number) => Effect.succeed(n)
export const shouldNotReport2 = double(wrapInEffect(regularFunction(5)))

// Should trigger diagnostic: proper pipeable chain with correct types
const addHundred = (e: Effect.Effect<number>) => Effect.map(e, (n) => n + 100)
export const shouldReport3 = addOne(7).pipe(double, addHundred, triple)

// Should NOT report: already using pipe
export const shouldNotReport3 = addOne(5).pipe(double, toString)
export const shouldNotReport4 = addOne(5).pipe(double)
export const shouldNotReport5 = addOne(5).pipe(multiplyBy(2))

// Should trigger
export const shouldReport = Effect.runPromise(Effect.log("Hello"))
